plain_definition: VARA's category, under Dubai's Virtual Asset Issuance Rulebook, for a token designed to hold a stable value by referencing one or more official currencies -- the Dubai equivalent of what other jurisdictions call a fiat-backed stablecoin. An FRVA referencing the UAE Dirham falls outside VARA's own regulatory scope and instead sits exclusively with the Central Bank of the UAE.
formal_definition: VARA's Virtual Asset (VA) Issuance Rulebook contemplates Asset Reference Token (ART) issuance generally, with a dedicated annex, the Fiat-Referenced Virtual Assets Issuance Rules (FRVA Rules), setting FRVA-specific requirements. VARA states that an issuer requires either Registration with VARA pre-approval or a Full-fledged Regulated VARA Licence prior to issuance. VARA has separately clarified that an FRVA designed to maintain a stable value in relation to the UAE Dirham is designated to be under the exclusive purview of the UAE's Central Bank, not VARA.
